name: Stem cell rejuvenation surveillance pass for June 9, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Stem Cell Rejuvenation since the 2026-05-31 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a delta surveillance pass from the 2026-05-31T16:42:37Z public baseline publication through 2026-06-09. The local scoped trial audit found zero existing trial-watch records for this track. PubMed Entrez-date searches found no same-window Cdc42/CASIN, p38/MuSC, or NAD/stem-cell result that changes the public interpretation; the broad stem-cell-rejuvenation search returned same-window context records including a mouse 15-PGDH muscle-repair paper, an aged-bone MMP9 neutralization paper, and a neural-crest review, but these do not add in vivo human stem-cell rejuvenation evidence or alter the human_biomarker_signal stage. ClinicalTrials.gov broad search surfaced NCT06877377, a not-yet-recruiting hDNAgr hematopoietic-stem-cell aging registry with no posted results and a 2025-07-20 last update. Because that registry predates the baseline and is a source-completeness issue rather than a new field delta, no public source, study, finding, outlook, activity item, or candidate bundle is warranted in this ordinary surveillance pass. A first internal coverage assessment was added and recommends a coverage_repair pass before the next ordinary surveillance rotation.

materiality_decision.rationale: The post-publication delta did not produce a result-bearing source that changes stage, momentum, read firmness, safety framing, or outlook wording. However, discovering a pre-baseline not-yet-recruiting aging HSC registry means the active-trials category is incomplete and should be repaired through a scoped coverage pass rather than treated as ordinary same-window field progress.

search_log.summary: The local scoped report contained zero registry-linked interventional studies and no current local trial-watch candidates.

search_log.query: stem cell rejuvenation, Entrez-date window 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-09
search_log.summary: Returned PMIDs 42256061, 42249716, 42231151, 42230256, 42228536, 42224774, and 42220642. The close records were review-level, animal/context, adjacent tissue-repair, wrong-scope, or not result-bearing for in vivo human stem-cell rejuvenation.

search_log.query: Cdc42 CASIN hematopoietic stem cell aging; p38 MAPK muscle stem cell aging regeneration; NAD stem cell aging, Entrez-date window 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-09
search_log.summary: Targeted Cdc42/CASIN, p38/MuSC, and NAD/stem-cell searches returned no same-window PubMed records that change the public baseline.

search_log.query: stem cell rejuvenation aging; CASIN Cdc42 hematopoietic stem cell aging; muscle stem cell satellite cell aging exercise older adult; NAD stem cell aging older adult; hDNAgr hematopoietic stem cell aging
search_log.summary: Found one relevant missing registry, NCT06877377, for hDNAgr hematopoietic-stem-cell aging therapy; it is not yet recruiting, has no posted results, and was last updated before the public baseline. Other close records were old withdrawn exercise/satellite-cell, NAD-overlap, sarcopenia, or cosmetic/skin-aging records.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-09 filtered for stem cell, satellite cell, hematopoietic, Cdc42, CASIN, p38, MMP9, 15-PGDH, NAD, and rejuvenation terms
search_log.summary: Returned only false-positive or wrong-scope preprint records in the same-window screen; no direct stem-cell-rejuvenation preprint changed materiality.

search_log.query: NCT06877377 hDNAgr Therapy S.LAB Soloways aging hematopoietic stem cells; Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Natural Genome Reconstruction Technology; hDNAgr therapy aging hematopoietic stem cells
search_log.summary: Search results led back to the ClinicalTrials.gov registry, Cochrane CENTRAL mirroring, and older hDNAgr background articles; no posted result or independent primary clinical outcome source was found.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Not-yet-recruiting randomized blinded placebo-controlled hDNAgr hematopoietic-stem-cell aging registry in adults 45 to 65, with safety, telomere, blood-count, CD34-positive progenitor, biological-age, SF-36, and inflammatory endpoints; no posted results and last registry update predates the public baseline.

excluded_sources.title: Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Natural Genome Reconstruction Technology of Hematopoietic Stem Cells in Adult Patients
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant missing active-registry context, but it is not yet recruiting, has no posted results, and predates the 2026-05-31 baseline; handle through coverage repair rather than ordinary same-window surveillance.

excluded_sources.title: 15-PGDH inhibition promotes muscle repair and strength recovery during GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced weight loss.
excluded_sources.reason: Mouse obesity, semaglutide, and injury-repair context with muscle stem-cell function and strength endpoints; adjacent to muscle repair but not an aged-human or direct stem-cell rejuvenation therapy result.

excluded_sources.title: In vivo circRNA-engineered macrophages mediate localized MMP9 neutralization to rejuvenate aged bone.
excluded_sources.reason: Aged-mouse skeletal-aging and macrophage/MMP9 immunotherapy context with stem-cell senescence readouts; decision-relevant for future taxonomy or niche/immune repair review, but not enough to change the current human biomarker stage.

excluded_sources.title: The neural crest across the lifespan: Implications for aging, degeneration, and regeneration.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level neural-crest aging and regenerative-medicine context; it does not add primary in vivo human evidence or alter the public support map.

excluded_sources.title: Rejuvenation of Aged Muscle Stem Cells Through Exercise
excluded_sources.reason: Withdrawn older registry with no posted results; relevant to source completeness but not a current field delta.

excluded_sources.title: Effect of Nicotinamide Riboside and Pterostilbene Supplementation on Muscle Regeneration in Elderly Humans
excluded_sources.reason: Completed no-results registry that overlaps NAD and muscle-regeneration questions; it predates this delta window and does not provide a stem-cell-specific public update.

notes: Triage selected this as rank 1 because it was the next distinct ready surveillance target after recently handled tracks.

The public outlook remains accurate enough for readers: human support is still ex vivo or biomarker-level, functional evidence remains preclinical, and no posted in vivo human stem-cell rejuvenation results were found.
NCT06877377 is important because it contradicts a zero-trial local watchlist, but it does not by itself support a stage, momentum, or confidence change.

PMID 42220642 and PMID 42228536 should be considered during a later taxonomy or coverage pass because they sit near the border between stem-cell rejuvenation, niche support, immune modulation, and tissue repair.
next_actions: Run coverage_repair for Stem Cell Rejuvenation before another ordinary surveillance pass, focused on NCT06877377, older withdrawn/no-results registries, recent reviews, seminal anchors, and taxonomy boundaries.
If coverage repair stages NCT06877377, treat it as no-results active-registry context unless posted results or a linked publication appears.
Continue ordinary surveillance only after the active-registry and review/source-completeness gaps are resolved or explicitly excluded.
